pattern: Whether a word starts and ends with a vowel is a fixed per-word fact, so a prefix-sum array of that 0/1 flag turns every range query into an O(1) subtraction.
hints: Whether a given word qualifies (starts and ends with a vowel) never changes across queries, so compute that flag for every word exactly once.
Build a running prefix sum of the qualifying flags so the count over any index range becomes a difference of two prefix totals.
For a query [l, r], the answer is prefix[r + 1] minus prefix[l], where prefix[i] holds the count of qualifying words among the first i words.

edge_inputs.description: single word, single-index query on it
single-character words where the same letter is both the start and end
query range of length one landing on a non-qualifying word
many overlapping queries over the same small array

stress.description: random words of random length and case-fixed vowel/consonant edges with random query ranges
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 20000))
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        length = rng.randint(1, 40)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(length)))
    q = max(1, min(n, 5000))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        l = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        r = rng.randint(l, n - 1)
        queries.append([l, r])
    return [words, queries]
